title: senecio viscosus
intro: Senecio viscosus, also known as sticky groundsel, is a perennial herb native to the Mediterranean region, characterized by its sticky leaves and yellow flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Senecio viscosus include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate congestion, and soothe coughs, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of Senecio viscosus can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be used in topical applications, such as salves and poultices, to treat skin irritations and wounds.
